page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 6455B processors running at a high clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong computational and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as in Passmark database operations and bzip3 single-threaded decompression. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks fall into the strong category, supported by the high-speed DDR5 memory. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions help deliver top-tier prompt processing speeds for medium and large LLM models, though text generation for large models drops to average speeds.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7iz.16xlarge offers a high-density memory configuration paired with high-frequency processing, reducing the trade-off typically seen between memory capacity and single-core compute speed. However, the lack of bundled local NVMe storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on network storage, which may introduce latency. This instance is suitable for memory-intensive applications requiring high CPU clock speeds, such as relational databases, data compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6455B processors on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, Passmark database operations, and medium-to-large LLM prompt processing, aided by Intel AMX instructions. Conversely, large-model text generation and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression show average performance. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads requiring high-frequency compute, such as relational databases, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier database and single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized virtual server combining 512 GB of DDR5 RAM with 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processors for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6455B processors
512.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier performance in database operations and Geekbench benchmarks
Intel AMX support delivering strong CPU-based LLM inference speeds
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for relational databases, software compilation, and memory-intensive applications
